name: Graham Crackers
recipeIngredient: 1/2 cup Flour, rye; plus additional for rolling out the dough
3 tbsp Butter, unsalted; chilled and cut into small bits
recipeInstructions:
1. Combine the all-purpose flour, whole-wheat flour, rye flour, sugar, salt, baking soda, baking powder, and cinnamon in a bowl.
2. Add the butter and shortening. Mix with your fingertips or a pastry blender until small, even particles are formed.
3. In a separate bowl, mix together the honey, molasses, cold water, and vanilla extract.
4. Gradually add the liquid mixture to the dry ingredients, tossing with a fork until evenly incorporated.
5. Press the dough into a ball. It may be crumbly, but do not add additional water. Wrap in plastic wrap and chill for several hours or overnight.
6. Preheat the oven to 350°F (175°C).
7. Halve the chilled dough. Let soften for about 15 minutes.
8. Sprinkle rye flour on a sheet of waxed paper. Place one piece of dough on top and flatten with a rolling pin, using even pressure.
9. Sprinkle lightly with rye flour and place another sheet of waxed paper on top. Roll out to form a rectangle about 7x15 inches.
10. Prick the dough all over at intervals of 1/2-1 inch using a skewer, fork, or puff-pastry pricker.
11. Cut into squares approximately 2-1/2 inches.
12. Transfer the squares to an ungreased baking sheet with a spatula, placing them close together.
13. Repeat with remaining dough. Re-roll scraps and cut additional squares.
14. Bake in the middle of the oven for about 15 minutes or until lightly browned on edges. Remove from oven and cool completely before storing airtight. Keep at least 24 hours before serving.
